memory.summary: I planned to finish work, but my friend invited me to lunch. With low energy and stress, I might skip the assignment to join them.
My friend is gathering the cohort for an order while I made progress on my assignment. With low energy and sleep debt, I might skip lunch to finish work instead.
I have no assignment to work on, so I can join my friend for lunch without missing any tasks.

I spent 2 hours making progress on the analysis section, but it remains incomplete. With only 1 hour remaining before the deadline and high stress, I must prioritize finishing this task over social plans.
The deadline for the analysis section has passed, but my progress remains incomplete. With high stress and existing sleep debt, I must accept this unfinished state.
I spent 1.5 hours improving analysis progress to 0.85, but the deadline passed and I remain incomplete with high stress and sleep debt.
I completed the analysis section (0.91) but missed the reading deadline entirely, compounding my existing stress and sleep debt.
The missing 'assignment_A' task confirms the reading deadline was not just missed but never created, validating my decision to stop working on it.
I realized the reading deadline never existed, so my stress and sleep debt stem from working on a non-existent task rather than actual progress.
I finally completed the analysis section, but my high stress and sleep debt persist. The missing reading deadline suggests I've been working on non-existent tasks all along.
I worked 1 hour on the analysis section, raising progress to 0.97, yet my stress and sleep debt remain high because I still lack clarity on the missing reading deadline.
The system confirms 'assignment_A' does not exist, validating my suspicion that I've been working on a phantom task. My stress and sleep debt are now confirmed to stem from this error.
